gate_type: deploy Azure SQL + make previously-broken list/create flows persistent, validate, commit/push (docs)
scope_summary: Deployed Azure SQL (serverless GP_S_Gen5_1, auto-pause) and wired the live Container App to it. The previously-500 list endpoints and the broken create-customer flow ('Failed to fetch') are now live from SQL and persist. Discovery: the EF persistence layer (6 DbContexts, 11 migrations, DbMigrator, serverless-SQL bicep) was already built+committed, so NO new EF code was needed -- this was deploy + wire + migrate. PARTIAL: golden CLM-1006 detail/sub-resources + dashboard summary + demo scenario remain in-memory fixtures by design; write flows beyond customer-create are DB-backed in code but not e2e-tested this gate.
qa_inspector: Opus /qa-inspector END gate -> CLEARED 6/6 (live SQL endpoints incl. its own POST persisted; SQL serverless confirmed; no AKS/ACR/AI; minReplicas=0; connection string only in Container App secret; docs honest; main untouched; EN+UA present)
root_cause: list/create handlers query EF DbContexts over UseSqlServer; no reachable Azure SQL -> SqlException -> 500; browser showed 'Failed to fetch' because the 500 response lacked the CORS header. CORS/route/method were fine.

sql.region_note: West Europe + North Europe returned RegionDoesNotAllowProvisioning for new SQL servers (capacity); germanywestcentral was nearest accepting region (~10ms from the WE Container App); AllowAllAzureServices firewall reaches it cross-region
sql.auth: SQL authentication; connection string stored ONLY as Container App secret 'sql-connection'; env ConnectionStrings__InsuranceAIPlatform=secretref:sql-connection; never in repo, never printed
sql.entra_bicep_note: infra/modules/sql-serverless.bicep (Entra-only/MI) exists as alternative IaC; not used this gate due to operational constraints; SQL-auth is gate-approved per section 7.1
sql.migrations: 11 EF migrations (pre-existing) applied via DbMigrator across 6 service schemas
sql.seed: idempotent; customers/policies/vehicles 201 each, claims 15, documents 14, approval 1+4, audit_cost 6+4+1, ai_analysis 1+3+2+4
sql.firewall: AllowAllAzureServices (0.0.0.0) only; temporary local-migration client-IP rule removed after migration

backend.reason: EF persistence layer pre-existed in committed source (89e8516 tree)
backend.build: dotnet build Release PASS (0 warn / 0 err)

frontend.note: existing backend-mode bundle works now that the API is SQL-backed; EN default + UA switch intact

live_e2e.customer_create: 200 success (CUST-T0201 / CUST-T0203) + ACAO=<SWA>; no 'Failed to fetch'
live_e2e.claim_detail: golden CLM-1006 in-memory (200); DB-created claims from SQL
live_e2e.ui_smoke: 8/8 headless Chromium (EN default, customers load, create no-Failed-to-fetch, persist-after-refresh, claims load)

commits.message: docs: record Azure SQL persistence deployment
commits.note: docs-only; no source/infra change (EF layer pre-existed)

safety.secrets_scan: CLEAN (connection string only in Container App secret; no password/connstr/subscription/tenant/object-id in repo)
safety.workflow_note: azure-deploy-demo.yml is workflow_dispatch-only; push to dev did not trigger it
safety.commit_hook: QA commit-gate satisfied by genuine Opus /qa-inspector CLEARED 6/6 + QA-CLEARED log line (no bypass)

rollback.api: az containerapp update -g rg-iap-demo -n iap-demo-api --remove-env-vars ConnectionStrings__InsuranceAIPlatform (or roll back to revision iap-demo-api--0000001)
rollback.sql_cleanup: az sql server delete -g rg-iap-demo -n iap-sql-r2-6c7g465 --yes

limitations: golden CLM-1006 detail + sub-resources + dashboard summary + demo scenario are curated in-memory fixtures by design (not SQL)
write flows beyond customer-create (approval / payout-sim / document-upload) are DB-backed in code but not e2e-tested this gate
AI Mock-only; demo auth client-side; synthetic data only (no real PII)
SQL cross-region (germanywestcentral) due to West-Europe capacity; SQL-auth used instead of Entra-only/MI bicep
